operating_state: Microsoft Cloud revenue increased 24% to $33.7 billion driven by Azure growth of 30% and Office 365 Commercial growth of 17%. Office Commercial revenue increased 15% while Consumer revenue increased 5% with Microsoft 365 subscribers reaching 78.4 million. LinkedIn revenue increased 9%, Dynamics revenue increased 21% driven by Dynamics 365 growth of 27%, and Windows revenue increased 9% with OEM growth of 11%. Devices revenue decreased 9%.
cost_structure: Primary expenses relate to employee compensation, cloud infrastructure and datacenter operations, product development, and marketing activities. The company continues investing in cloud infrastructure and AI-powered platforms to support competitive positioning and solution delivery across segments.
material_events: Microsoft completed the Activision Blizzard acquisition on October 13, 2023 for $95.00 per share in cash. The company conducted exchange offers for Activision's outstanding notes, completing early settlement on November 6, 2023 by issuing $3.4 billion in new Microsoft notes. Microsoft received IRS Notices seeking $28.9 billion in additional tax payments plus penalties for tax years 2004-2013 related to intercompany transfer pricing.
macro_exposures: The company faces material transfer pricing disputes with the IRS covering tax years 2004-2013 with proposed adjustments of $28.9 billion plus penalties and interest. Microsoft disagrees with the proposed adjustments and will contest through administrative appeals and potential judicial proceedings.
forward_outlook: Microsoft does not expect final resolution of the IRS transfer pricing issues within the next 12 months and does not anticipate significant changes to tax contingencies for these matters in that timeframe. The company continues focusing on AI-powered platforms and tools to deliver solutions supporting business competitiveness and operational efficiency.
uncertainty_profile: The primary uncertainty involves the ongoing IRS examination of intercompany transfer pricing for tax years 2004-2013 with potential exposure of $28.9 billion plus penalties and interest. The company believes its current tax contingency allowances are adequate but faces extended administrative and potential judicial proceedings with uncertain timing and outcomes.
